creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 232426
Patch Makefile to respect CC

One problem: The Makefile does not respect CC. It overrides CC from the environment with PCC, which is getting set to gcc, even though the port does not declare gcc as a build dependency.

I think this patch achieves what you're after while respecting CC provided by the ports system.

Another approach would be a simpler Makefile that just does what it's told and moving all the decision making to a wrapper script that feeds the Makefile the proper CC, CFLAGS, etc.  I've found that the shell is generally better at this than Make.  Hence then existence of GNU configure, cmake, etc., though they are usually overkill and more trouble than they're worth.

E.g. https://github.com/auerlab/biolibc/blob/master/cave-man-install.sh

Package managers would just use the Makefile directly, while the script can be used for ad hoc builds.
